scenario: Single-file apply_patch on small file — new shape

given.patch:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: tmp/small.py
@@
-print('a')
+print('b')
*** End Patch

scenario: Multi-file apply_patch — new shape on all entries

given.patch: valid multi-file patch touching a.py, b.py, c.py
single small hunk inside large.py (1 line change)

scenario: Tool part serialized to LLM — invariant

parts: one apply_patch part with new shape

emittedModelMessage.tool_result_content: equals state.output string (success message); contains no substring from state.metadata.files; contains no occurrence of 'before' or 'after' field names

scenario: UI renders new-shape apply_patch part — happy path

then.domQuery: diff component rendered with non-empty hunk content

scenario: UI renders legacy-shape apply_patch part — backwards compatibility

then.domQuery: diff component rendered with non-empty content
then.note: Code path reads file.diff regardless of presence/absence of before/after.

scenario: Dreaming pruner — legacy-shape fixture still pruned

then.fakePart.state.metadata.files[0].before: contains 'dream-pruned: dropped 300,000 bytes of file before snapshot'
then.fakePart.state.metadata.files[0].after: contains 'dream-pruned: dropped 300,000 bytes of file after snapshot'

scenario: Move-type patch — movePath preserved, no before/after

given.patch:
*** Begin Patch
*** Rename File: old/x.py -> new/x.py
*** End Patch

metadata.files[0].diff: present (may be empty if no content change)
